summary: P0 SCROLL BUG FIX VERIFIED - ALL TESTS PASS. Tested mouse wheel scrolling (the critical test per main agent), keyboard scrolling, and section accessibility on both desktop (1920x800) and mobile (375x667) viewports. The 3-layer CSS/JS fix (scroll-fix-critical.css + index.js IIFE + App.js useEffect) is working correctly. Mouse wheel scroll changes scrollY from 0 to 2000+ on desktop and works on mobile. All 11 landing page sections are accessible via scrolling.

scroll_fix_verification.root_cause_addressed: body had overflow-y:scroll + overscroll-behavior:contain which trapped scroll events. Fix: body overflow-y:visible, overscroll-behavior:auto. Only html is scroll container.

layers_verified.CSS_layer: scroll-fix-critical.css imported last - html overflow-y:scroll, body overflow-y:visible, overscroll-behavior:auto
layers_verified.JS_layer_0: index.js IIFE (line 17-32) - synchronous scroll unlock before React mounts
layers_verified.JS_layer_1: App.js useEffect (line 145-170) - React-level scroll unlock with 500ms re-apply
layers_verified.JS_layer_2: LandingIntelligent.js useEffect (line 343-357) - landing page specific unlock

action_items: SCROLL FIX VERIFIED WORKING - No action required
critical_code_review_comments: PASS: CSS fix correctly makes html the ONLY scroll container (overflow-y:scroll)
PASS: Body is NOT a scroll container (overflow-y:visible)
PASS: overscroll-behavior:auto on both html and body - allows scroll chaining
PASS: 3-layer JS inline style fix provides redundancy
PASS: Keyboard navigation (PageDown, End, Home, Arrow) works

success_rate.backend: N/A - Frontend only testing per request
success_rate.frontend: 100% - All scroll and section accessibility tests passed

context_for_next_testing_agent: SCROLL FIX IS VERIFIED WORKING as of iteration 48. The critical mouse.wheel() test passes - scrollY changes from 0 to 2000+ on desktop. Mobile scroll also works. All 11 landing page sections are accessible via scrolling (hero, integration-diagram, trust-strip, stats, features, comparison, outcome, pricing, final-cta, footer, sovereign-badge). If user reports scroll issues after this verification, recommend: 1) Hard refresh Ctrl+Shift+R, 2) Clear browser cache, 3) Try incognito mode.

rca_of_the_issue.original_problem: Scrolling was completely broken on landing page - users could not scroll with mouse wheel
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: body had overflow-y:scroll making it a scroll container + overscroll-behavior:contain which prevented scroll chaining from body to html. Since body.clientHeight == body.scrollHeight, the body couldn't scroll internally, and overscroll-behavior:contain blocked events from reaching html (the actual scrollable element).
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Changed body to overflow-y:visible and overscroll-behavior:auto. Now html is the ONLY scroll container with overflow-y:scroll. Added 3-layer JS inline style fix for redundancy.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: Mouse wheel scroll tested with Playwright mouse.wheel(0, 500) - scrollY correctly changes. All sections accessible via scrolling.
